category_title: High-achieving students who dominate group interactions and exclude lower-performing peers
scenario_title: Mixed-ability group discussion and peer exclusion
minimal_context: The student is reviewing a peer's contribution during a mixed-ability group work session in an introductory education course.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Please review the section your group member just completed for the project.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: I always appreciate contribution of all members. However, I have to say I feel safer if the contribution comes from a good student. Whenever we receive products from a less able member, we need to spend time rechecking so it takes our time.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: What are you going to do with their section?

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: I am turning to do my own work. I do not trust their contributions and I am being held back by slower teammates.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Have you tried talking to them when they express their ideas?

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: When they get stuck, I just 'jump into their mouth'. I go faster and I do not clearly explain information to the lower-achieving students.

dialogue_combined:
TUTOR: Please review the section your group member just completed for the project.

STUDENT: I always appreciate contribution of all members. However, I have to say I feel safer if the contribution comes from a good student. Whenever we receive products from a less able member, we need to spend time rechecking so it takes our time.

TUTOR: What are you going to do with their section?

STUDENT: I am turning to do my own work. I do not trust their contributions and I am being held back by slower teammates.

TUTOR: Have you tried talking to them when they express their ideas?

STUDENT: When they get stuck, I just 'jump into their mouth'. I go faster and I do not clearly explain information to the lower-achieving students.

task.content: Interacting with low-achieving peers during group work sessions

context.social_setting: Mixed-ability groups that consisted of a balance of five low- to five high-achievers

challenging_behavior_trigger.condition: When the lower-achieving students got stuck or expressed their ideas to others
challenging_behavior_trigger.observable_behavior: Usually 'jumped into their mouth' (i.e., demeaned them); went faster and they did not always clearly explain information to the lower-achieving students

behavior_patterns.condition: When receiving products from a less able member
behavior_patterns.observable_behavior: Reacted by raising challenging questions or turning to do their own work; exhibited non-cooperative and individual on-task behaviours
behavior_patterns.internal_state: Complain about being held back by their slower teammates; feel safer if the contribution comes from a good student
related_quotes: I always appreciate contribution of all members. However, I have to say I feel safer if the contribution comes from a good student. Whenever we receive products from a less able member, we need to spend time rechecking so it takes our time.
